rule.name: Azure AD New MFA Method Registered For User
rule.description: The following analytic detects the registration of a new Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) method for an Azure AD account. It leverages Azure AD AuditLogs to identify when a user registers new security information. This activity is significant because adversaries who gain unauthorized access to an account may add their own MFA method to maintain persistence. If confirmed malicious, this could allow attackers to bypass existing security controls, maintain long-term access, and potentially escalate their privileges within the environment.
rule.query:
`azure_monitor_aad` category=AuditLogs operationName="User registered security info" properties.operationType=Add
  | rename properties.* as *
  | rename targetResources{}.* as *
  | rename userAgent as user_agent
  | fillnull
  | stats count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime
    BY action dest user
       src vendor_account vendor_product
       user_agent result resultDescription
       signature
  | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
  | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
  | `azure_ad_new_mfa_method_registered_for_user_filter`
false_positives: Newly onboarded users who are registering an MFA method for the first time will also trigger this detection.
